channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Trump Promised to Tear Up the Regime-Change Playbook. He Is Now Following It.
item.description: Exaggerate the threat and underestimate the consequences.

item.title: Trump Likely to Confront Democratic Wall on Iran War Funding
item.description: An emergency military spending request to continue the Iran strikes may soon be sent to U.S lawmakers.

item.title: Iran Threatens to Stop Oil From Leaving the Middle East
item.description: Trump has warned that doing so will only escalate the U.S. military’s war effort.

item.title: State Department Refuses Former Staffers’ Help in Middle East Evacuation
item.description: Around 250 State Department employees could assist with efforts.

item.title: Why China Might Have a Role to Play in Iran
item.description: Diplomatic messaging suggests Beijing’s potential for mediation in the war.

item.title: China’s Middle East Ties Go Far Beyond Iran
item.description: Beijing is as concerned about Gulf partners as Tehran.

item.title: Why Netanyahu Chose to Strike Iran Now
item.description: After threatening war for decades, the Israeli leader saw his window of opportunity closing.

item.title: Can Beijing Help Contain Washington’s War?
item.description: Donald Trump may need Xi Jinping’s help to stop conflict from spreading.

item.title: The Trump Administration Is Driving Migrants Into Traffickers’ Hands
item.description: Irregular migration needs a compassionate and careful approach.

item.title: The Troubling Ethics of Crowdfunding
item.description: While it is encouraging to see many people responding to the appeals of strangers in great need, there are fundamental ethical problems with crowdfunding, especially when it becomes a primary method of helping others. Systemic bias and fraud associated with platforms like GoFundMe are only the start.

item.title: The Middle-Power Moment?
item.description: With American and Chinese behavior causing unease globally, the world's middle powers know that opportunities to defend their own interests will not remain open forever. But whether and how effectively such a diverse grouping can mobilize itself very much remains to be seen.

item.title: Trump’s ‘Shield’ Against Multilateralism
item.description: The Shield of the Americas summit marked a turning point in Western Hemisphere diplomacy.

item.title: Are We Facing an AI Nightmare?
item.description: AI-doom scenarios are going viral because there are no easy public-policy responses to the problem of large-scale but not universal technological unemployment. Society’s best hope is a Goldilocks scenario, where the AI rollout is not too fast, and where the industry is not too oligopolistic.

item.title: So This, Finally, Is the ‘Trump Doctrine’
item.description: A property developer who sought to remake skylines in his image is now doing the same to the global order.

item.title: Kim Yo Jong Condemns South Korea-US Joint Military Exercises
item.description: The powerful sister of North Korean leader labeled the “Freedom Shield” exercises as war rehearsal while warning of overwhelming deterrent response.

item.title: EU, Thailand Must Address Migrant Worker Rights in Trade Talks
item.description: In its rush to diversify trading partners amid growing global uncertainty, the EU should not sacrifice the potential of trade negotiations to improve rights and address abusive practices.

item.title: Albert Hirschman Strikes Back
item.description: The globalization to which we have grown accustomed and prospered from has survived recessions, financial crises, and a worldwide pandemic, but it may not survive today's escalating geoeconomic conflict. A book published more than 80 years ago – and all but ignored at the time – explains why.

item.title: The Paris Prelude: How Quiet Diplomacy Is Shaping the Coming Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: This weekend’s meetings between China’s vice premier and the U.S. treasury secretary and trade representative will help determine the outcomes of the big summit in April.

item.title: The War in Iran Sends Economic Shockwaves Through Central Asia
item.description: Iran’s ban on food exports and the disruption of trade corridors are hitting the landlocked economies of Central Asia at a vulnerable moment.

item.title: Epstein and the Globalization of Criminal Exploitation
item.description: While the Jeffrey Epstein affair continues to receive global attention, the true scale of human trafficking and modern slavery remains underappreciated, and the perpetrators almost always go unpunished. G20 countries can start cracking down on this scourge immediately by focusing on six priorities.

item.title: Indonesia’s Civil-Military Boundaries Under New Pressure
item.description: The plan to train thousands of civil servants as National Reserve troops is drawing criticism over risks of militarizing the bureaucracy.

item.title: A Primer on North Korea’s Upcoming Parliamentary Election
item.description: Of course these elections have no real competition. But they are not meaningless.

item.title: Modernizing the South Korea-US Alliance Requires Checking the Assumptions
item.description: Both Seoul and Washington want to “modernize” the alliance – but they have different goals in mind.

item.title: China’s 5-Year Plan Has Moved Beyond the Chip War. Washington Hasn’t Noticed.
item.description: Primary-source analysis of the 15th Five-Year Plan reveals a computing infrastructure strategy already being deployed in Southeast Asia, with explicit military-civil fusion mandates that current export controls do not address.

item.title: Unmasking the Hypocrisy Behind Kazakhstan NGO Foreign Funding Law
item.description: Foreign capital is celebrated as a driver of progress by the state, while foreign-funded civil society is painted as a threat.

item.title: Russia’s European Enclave Is Its Soft Underbelly
item.description: Kaliningrad was a strategic beachhead, but it is now a major vulnerability.

item.title: India’s Promising New Counter-Terrorism Strategy
item.description: India's new counter-terrorism strategy seeks to replace the reactive and disjointed approach of recent decades with a holistic doctrine that supports unity and coordination across India’s vast and often fragmented security apparatus. But the path from an eight-page document to a safer reality is littered with practical hurdles.

item.title: Why Trump Should Be Careful What He Wishes for in Cuba
item.description: A manufactured crisis could lead to state collapse, with lasting consequences for the United States.

item.title: How the Iran War Hurts Thailand and Singapore
item.description: Spiraling natural gas prices leave both countries exposed.

item.title: Resilience Will Decide the Iran War
item.description: Wars of this kind aren’tnecessarily won by the side that’s more powerful.

item.title: Africa After Aid
item.description: Cuts have revealed the continent's economic resilience.

item.title: What Is the Endgame in Iran?
item.description: Trump needs to figure out what he wants—and quickly.

item.title: The Trouble With State Capitalism
item.description: America is adopting a risky model.

item.title: Myanmar’s Military on the Back Foot Over Fuel Shortages
item.description: But the junta is setting up a new propaganda unit in order to send more positive messages.

item.title: Indonesia Announces Social Media Ban For Children Under 16
item.description: From March 28, young Indonesians will be blocked from having accounts on “high-risk” digital platforms, including YouTube, TikTok, and Instagram.

item.title: Indonesia Says It Has Finalized BrahMos Missile Purchase From India
item.description: The country will become the second Southeast Asian nation to acquire the powerful Indo-Russian weapons system, after the Philippines.

item.title: Inside North Korea’s 9th Party Congress
item.description: What happened at the latest congress of the Workers' Party of Korea and why does it matter?

item.title: The Broken Japarov-Tashiev Tandem
item.description: Moral panic, memory, and symbolic politics in Kyrgyzstan.
